case_id.description: Maestro case id; threaded onto every RedactionProposal.
case_id.title: Case Id

jurisdiction.description: Legal regime; only 'federal_foia' in MVP, passed as a real param.
jurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction

records.description: The candidate records to review (from the record-store fan-out).

items.description:
A record returned by a department repository (§2 stage 3, §10, §8.4).

`content_hash` (sha256 of the source bytes) starts the release-integrity
chain (§8.4) — every later hash is verified back toward it. `is_responsive`
is ``None`` until the Review agent decides; ``True``/``False`` afterwards.

case_id.description: Maestro case instance id; ties every contract to one case.
case_id.title: Case Id

jurisdiction.description: Legal regime. Only 'federal_foia' in MVP; kept a str so new regimes are additive.
jurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction

record_ref.description: Stable id of the record within its repository.
record_ref.title: Record Ref

department.description: Department repository the record came from.
department.title: Department

content_hash.description: sha256 of source bytes. Starts the §8.4 integrity chain. Strict: never coerce.
content_hash.title: Content Hash

items.title: CandidateRecord

records.title: Records

officer.description: Identity recorded on each ApprovedRedaction.officer and used as the default Action Center assignee for the §5 approval task. Stamped on the decision so the audit trail names who approved; the authoritative approval_token still comes from the completed task identity, not this field.
officer.title: Officer

approved.description: Officer decisions after the §5 human gate. Only 'approved'/'edited' entries carry release-bound bytes (approval_token + approved_content_hash, §8.4); 'rejected' entries are recorded for audit/corrections but release their content (disclosure default).

items.description:
An officer's decision on one proposed redaction (§5, §8.4, §10).

Carries the proposal evidence plus the human decision. `approval_token` and
`approved_content_hash` are the §8.4 inputs the release-integrity guard
consumes — both Strict so a missing/garbled token cannot coerce into a
truthy value. `edited_span` is set only when `decision == "edited"`.

pack_version.description: Pack semantic version the decision was grounded in.
pack_version.title: Pack Version

case_id.description: Maestro case instance id; ties every contract to one case.
case_id.title: Case Id

jurisdiction.description: Legal regime. Only 'federal_foia' in MVP; kept a str so new regimes are additive.
jurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction

span.description:
A located region inside a record (the unit a redaction or correction targets).

`unit` defaults to ``"char"``; ``"token"`` and ``"page_bbox"`` are carried so a
future document-understanding backing can address regions natively without a
contract change.

record_ref.description: Stable id of the record this span lives in.
record_ref.title: Record Ref

span.title: Span

rule_id.description: Grounding rule id from the proposal. Strict.
rule_id.title: Rule Id

rationale.description: Rationale from the proposal (officer may have noted edits separately).
rationale.title: Rationale

test_result.description:
Generic, data-driven result of applying a rule's legal test (spec item 4).

`elements` is keyed by element name; the *required* names come from
`Rule.required_test_elements`, never hardcoded here. The §8.3 completeness
check lives in ``shared.contracts.validation`` as a pure function, not a
model validator, because a failure is control flow (re-prompt OR route to
human) that sits above the type.

test.description: Which legal-test family was applied (mirrors the Rule.test).
test.title: Test

additionalProperties.description:
One filled element of a legal test (§8.1b, §8.3).

`hedged=True` or an empty `value`/`evidence` marks the element as incomplete,
which the confidence derivation reads as low confidence and the §8.3 validator
reads as a completeness violation.

value.description: The agent's finding for this element.
value.title: Value

hedged.description: True if the model hedged/was uncertain on this element.
hedged.title: Hedged

additionalProperties.title: TestElement

test_result.title: ExemptionTestResult

decision.description: Officer decision. 'rejected'/'edited' feed the corrections log (§7) and revise loop (§2).
decision.title: Decision

decided_at.description: When the decision was made (Clock seam).
decided_at.title: Decided At

edited_span.description:
A located region inside a record (the unit a redaction or correction targets).

`unit` defaults to ``"char"``; ``"token"`` and ``"page_bbox"`` are carried so a
future document-understanding backing can address regions natively without a
contract change.

record_ref.description: Stable id of the record this span lives in.
record_ref.title: Record Ref

quote.description: Optional verbatim text of the span, for human review / audit.
quote.title: Quote

edited_span.title: Span

approval_token.description: Token tied to this specific human approval (§8.4). Strict: never coerce.
approval_token.title: Approval Token

approved_content_hash.description: sha256 of the exact approved post-redaction bytes (§8.4). Strict.
approved_content_hash.title: Approved Content Hash

items.title: ApprovedRedaction

approved.title: Approved

proposals.description: Every proposed withholding across all records (each §8.3-validated, confidence DERIVED). Audit trail.

items.description:
A proposed withholding from the Review & Redaction agent (§3, §8.1, §8.3, §10).

The hero contract. Every field that grounds a withholding is here: the
`rule_id` (validated ∈ the PolicyProvider's returned set, §8.3), the
`citation` and `rationale` (source-grounded, foreseeable-harm), the generic
`test_result`, and the DERIVED `confidence`. `retrieved_corrections` are
advisory only and surface into human review (§7); they never set rule_id or
confidence.

pack_version.description: Pack semantic version the decision was grounded in.
pack_version.title: Pack Version

case_id.description: Maestro case instance id; ties every contract to one case.
case_id.title: Case Id

jurisdiction.description: Legal regime. Only 'federal_foia' in MVP; kept a str so new regimes are additive.
jurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction

record_ref.description: Record this redaction applies to (validated in-scope, §8.3).
record_ref.title: Record Ref

span.description:
A located region inside a record (the unit a redaction or correction targets).

`unit` defaults to ``"char"``; ``"token"`` and ``"page_bbox"`` are carried so a
future document-understanding backing can address regions natively without a
contract change.

record_ref.description: Stable id of the record this span lives in.
record_ref.title: Record Ref

span.title: Span

rule_id.description: PolicyProvider rule id grounding the withholding. Validated ∈ returned set (§8.3). Strict.
rule_id.title: Rule Id

citation.description: Statutory citation copied from the grounding Rule.
citation.title: Citation

test_result.description:
Generic, data-driven result of applying a rule's legal test (spec item 4).

`elements` is keyed by element name; the *required* names come from
`Rule.required_test_elements`, never hardcoded here. The §8.3 completeness
check lives in ``shared.contracts.validation`` as a pure function, not a
model validator, because a failure is control flow (re-prompt OR route to
human) that sits above the type.

test.description: Which legal-test family was applied (mirrors the Rule.test).
test.title: Test

additionalProperties.description:
One filled element of a legal test (§8.1b, §8.3).

`hedged=True` or an empty `value`/`evidence` marks the element as incomplete,
which the confidence derivation reads as low confidence and the §8.3 validator
reads as a completeness violation.

value.description: The agent's finding for this element.
value.title: Value

hedged.description: True if the model hedged/was uncertain on this element.
hedged.title: Hedged

additionalProperties.title: TestElement

test_result.title: ExemptionTestResult

confidence.description:
DERIVED routing signal — never a field the LLM fills (spec item 5, §8.1).

Produced only by ``derive_confidence`` (``shared.contracts.confidence``),
which encodes the §8.1 priority order. Recorded on the proposal as evidence
of *why* the proposal routes the way it does.

level.description: Routing level: 'low' forces close scrutiny / full review.
level.title: Level

samples.description: Number of samples drawn for the exemption classification.
samples.title: Samples

agreement.description: Fraction of samples agreeing on the classification.
agreement.title: Agreement

confidence.title: ConfidenceSignal

retrieved_corrections.description: Advisory corrections surfaced into review (§7). Never authoritative.

items.description:
An officer correction stored with grounding in the corrections log (§7).

Advisory only: a stored correction informs a future proposal but can never
set `rule_id` or confidence or bypass the PolicyProvider / human gate. The
grounding fields exist so a future retrieval backing has something to
retrieve and surface into human review.

direction.description: Whether the officer removed an over-redaction or added a missed one.
direction.title: Direction

record_context.description:
Context used to retrieve advisory corrections (§7 CorrectionsMemory).

Intentionally lightweight: `record_type` + a `snippet` are enough for the
MVP exact-match/recency retrieval; `embedding` stays ``None`` until the
LlamaIndex stretch backing fills it. Carrying it now means the retrieval
seam has something to key on without a later contract change.
record_context.title: RecordContext

span.description:
A located region inside a record (the unit a redaction or correction targets).

`unit` defaults to ``"char"``; ``"token"`` and ``"page_bbox"`` are carried so a
future document-understanding backing can address regions natively without a
contract change.

record_ref.description: Stable id of the record this span lives in.
record_ref.title: Record Ref

span.title: Span

pack_version.description: PolicyProvider pack version at correction time.
pack_version.title: Pack Version

officer.description: Identity of the officer who made the correction.
officer.title: Officer

items.title: Correction

retrieved_corrections.title: Retrieved Corrections

items.title: RedactionProposal

proposals.title: Proposals

reviewed.description: Per-record responsiveness decisions (so Maestro can forward clean / non-responsive records).

items.description:
Per-record responsiveness decision echoed on the output (agent-local).

Maestro reads this to mark non-responsive records and to distinguish a
responsive record carrying ZERO proposals (a clean, fully-disclosable record —
the disclosure default) from a non-responsive one. It is NOT a new pipeline
contract: it only surfaces the `is_responsive` decision the agent made about
each input record (the locked `CandidateRecord.is_responsive` field is what
Maestro ultimately stamps; this echo carries the decision out of the graph).

is_responsive.description: The agent's responsiveness decision for this record.
is_responsive.title: Is Responsive

proposal_count.description: How many redactions were proposed for this record.
proposal_count.title: Proposal Count

items.title: RecordReview

reviewed.title: Reviewed
